name: George Barrow
abstract: George Barrow (September 25, 1921 – March 20, 2013) was an American jazz saxophonist who played both tenor and baritone.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: I did an album with him at The Bohemia. It was me, George Barrow, Mal Waldron and Willie Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Jimmy Owens on it, James Moody, Joe Farrell, George Barrow, Bobby Timmons, piano, Eric Gale, Ron Carter, Billy Higgins. [INTERVIEWEE] I don’t recall that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: Saxes - George Barrow, Harold Blanchard, Amos Gordon, Aaron Maxwell, Stretch Riley, Dick Sanford, Bobby Smith, J.B. Sims, Norris Turney.

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] 1955, March 30, Kenny Clarke, Ernie Wilkins, septet date with George Barrow—Detroiter? [INTERVIEWEE] Barrow…
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
